name: SCATTER - physics-draggable pile of works
ref: Palmer Studio draggable product grid (2025), tldraw tactile canvas
palette: bone #f2ede4 + ink; each card keeps its own media color
brief: All works are loose cards tossed on an off-white surface with slight rotations and natural stacking; you drag them around with momentum and spring physics. No structured grid, no scroll - pure spatial rearrangement. Click a card to pin it full-screen. On mobile, tap-to-expand works; stacks re-shuffle on shake (device motion API). The tactile interaction IS the gallery, not a wrapper around it.

name: FREQUENCY - audio-reactive ambient gallery
ref: Sonic design trend 2026 (Muzli/index.dev), Resonate music viz studios
palette: two-color near-black + electric violet #7b2fff; audio drives color pulse
brief: Each work has an ambient soundtrack loop (generated or recorded); the background morphs in real-time to frequency data - bass = slow bloom, highs = fine grain ripple. Works appear as you listen your way through, not scroll. A minimal waveform equalizer strip at the bottom is the only UI chrome. Works in silence (click to unmute shows a waveform icon). Mobile uses Web Audio API with silent fallback to standard scroll. Sound as spatial navigation, not decoration.

name: GOLDEN HOUR - palette shifts with real clock
ref: Context-reactive / biometric design trend 2026, Linear time-aware theming
palette: dynamic: 00-06h deep indigo, 06-10h warm amber, 10-16h bone, 16-19h vermilion, 19-24h navy
brief: The entire page palette, sky gradient, and cast-shadow angle are driven by the visitor's real local time-of-day. Pre-dawn: deep indigo with stars. Sunrise: amber bleed. Afternoon: clean editorial bone. Golden hour: long vermilion shadows. Night: navy with lit-window warmth. Works are always the same; the ambient mood around them changes. A clock-face scrubber in the corner lets you preview other hours. No scroll mechanics - vertical snap. Mobile-first, no WebGL needed.

name: RIBBON - single elastic tape you stretch to navigate
ref: Codrops easeReverse experiments 2025, Jeton scroll morphing UX (Awwwards)
palette: two-color near-black + warm cream #f5efe0; ribbon = one accent thread
brief: A single physical-feeling ribbon threads through all works vertically; drag it off-center and it stretches with spring tension, snapping to reveal the adjacent work on release. Works are mounted on the ribbon like polaroids on a string. The ribbon's tautness is visualized with a thin colored thread that bows and rebounds with actual spring math. Scroll-only fallback for reduced-motion. Mobile: flick-to-snap gesture. The physics IS the navigation - no buttons, no tabs.

name: DITHER - real-time 1-bit dithering shader on all media
ref: Niccolò Fanton real-time dithering shader (Codrops 2025 top trend), game-boy / e-ink aesthetic
palette: two-color: background #e8e4d8 (paper) + ink #1a1a1a; dither is the color system
brief: All media is run through a real-time WebGL 1-bit Bayer-dithering shader, reducing every image and video to a gorgeous ordered pixel grid like a 1989 Game Boy screen or an e-ink panel. Hover brightens the threshold to reveal color underneath. On click the dither dissolves away to full resolution. The dither is not a filter applied on export - it is live, reacting to mouse proximity. Tiny, fast, WASM-free. Mobile: static dithered JPGs with CSS reveal. Anti-AI-polish as a radical aesthetic position.

name: TICKER - works scroll as a broadcast ticker strip
ref: Bloomberg/Reuters visual language, motion-design broadcast lower-thirds
palette: two-color near-black #0a0a0a + broadcast amber #f4a000; monospace throughout
brief: A single auto-scrolling horizontal ticker loop runs continuously at mid-page - works appear as labeled slugs (MODEL -- PROMPT -- DURATION -- COST) that you click to halt the tape and expand inline above/below. A second slower ticker carries context tags. The loop speed responds to cursor position: push right to fast-forward, left to rewind. Pure CSS scroll animation, no JS scroll events. Mobile: two stacked tickers. Feels like watching the market open on a media-gen trading floor.

name: FOLD - CSS 3D fold/unfold page transitions
ref: Exat Typeface, KOKUYO (CSSDA 2025 nominees); CSS 3D perspective fold patterns
palette: bone #f0ece3 + deep cobalt #1d2c6b; paper crease = thin shadow
brief: Each work lives on a virtual sheet of paper; advancing to the next folds the page diagonally like origami, crease shadow sharpening as it pivots, then the new sheet unfolds to reveal the next work. Pure CSS 3D transform with perspective - no WebGL. Works float on the flat unfolded page with clean white space and a single caption. The fold axis changes per transition (top, diagonal, left) for variety. Haptic on mobile (navigator.vibrate on fold snap). Print-world physicality in a browser.

name: CONSTELLATION - works as nodes in a navigable star map
ref: Fauna Robotics spatial UI (Awwwards June 2026), node-graph spatial design trend
palette: near-black #050810 + ice blue #a8d4f5; stars = scattered white points
brief: Works are positioned as glowing nodes on a dark star-field canvas, connected by faint constellation lines that form recognizable shapes (one per theme cluster). You pan and zoom the field to explore. Hovering a node shows its prompt + thumbnail in a floating panel; clicking locks it and dims others. A legend maps constellation shapes to categories. Built on Canvas 2D (no WebGL), so fast and mobile-friendly with touch pan/pinch-zoom. Space exploration as creative taxonomy.

name: CLASSIFIED - heavy redaction bars reveal on interaction
ref: Inkwell narrative secrecy concept (Awwwards 2025), government document aesthetic, anti-design
brief: Works are presented as classified intelligence briefings: every title, prompt, and caption is blacked out with thick redaction bars and [REDACTED] stamps. Hover scrubs the bar away to reveal the text; click declassifies the full work with a stamp-lift animation. Visual language borrows directly from actual FOIA documents: monospace Courier, rubber-stamp watermarks, document numbers, classification headers. Tension and reward as an interaction model. Mobile: tap-to-unredact. Provocative, memorable, thematically apt for AI-generated content.

name: MORPHGRID - grid columns elastically reflow between layouts
ref: Dropbox Brand (CSSDA 2025 nominee), Adveris immersive navigation (2025), Codrops staggered 3D grid formations
palette: bone #f3efe8 + ink #111; works use natural media color
brief: Works live in a grid that morphs between radically different layouts on scroll: starts as a tightly packed mosaic, breathes into a single-column editorial, collapses to a strip, then fans into a radial spoke arrangement. Each transition is a smooth GSAP-driven reflow - cells animate to their new positions, sizes, and rotations. No page navigation, one continuous morphing surface. The grid's current layout name appears in micro-type in the corner as an orientation signal. Mobile: linear morph between two simpler layouts.
